understanding.goal_understanding: The goal is to complete each KA59 level by moving and positioning all selectable pieces so that every goal tile marked '+' is simultaneously occupied by a selectable piece; clearing all levels wins the game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement uses MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN to shift the currently selected piece in 3-pixels-per-cell increments, with walls/boundaries and adjacent objects sometimes blocking or altering the move; pushable objects can be pushed if directly adjacent in the movement direction and space permits. When multiple selectables exist, control is switched via CLICK on the exact pixel coordinates of another selectable, changing player.id/player.position; the movement.*_blocked flags only indicate something adjacent and don’t guarantee the move will fail.

history.summary: Turn 1: MOVE_RIGHT | Test whether the apparent wall collision is truly blocking or triggers a special interaction (wrap/teleport/slide) by attempting to move right.
history.reasoning: Test whether the apparent wall collision is truly blocking or triggers a special interaction (wrap/teleport/slide) by attempting to move right.

history.summary: Turn 2: CLICK | The current piece is blocked on all sides, so switch control to the other selectable to find a movable path toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: The current piece is blocked on all sides, so switch control to the other selectable to find a movable path toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 3: MOVE_RIGHT | Test whether the adjacent wall actually blocks movement or causes a wrap/teleport-style displacement when moving into it.
history.reasoning: Test whether the adjacent wall actually blocks movement or causes a wrap/teleport-style displacement when moving into it.

history.summary: Turn 4: MOVE_UP | Test whether the apparent surrounding wall actually blocks movement; moving up may reveal a passage around the vertical wall to the goal area.
history.reasoning: Test whether the apparent surrounding wall actually blocks movement; moving up may reveal a passage around the vertical wall to the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 5: CLICK | This selectable at [21,21] is boxed in by walls; switch back to the other selectable to navigate toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: This selectable at [21,21] is boxed in by walls; switch back to the other selectable to navigate toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 6: MOVE_LEFT | Test whether the adjacent "wall" actually blocks movement or can be passed through to return to the previous cell and potentially escape the enclosure.
history.reasoning: Test whether the adjacent "wall" actually blocks movement or can be passed through to return to the previous cell and potentially escape the enclosure.

history.summary: Turn 7: MOVE_RIGHT | Probe whether the selected piece can still advance right from this pocket/corridor toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: Probe whether the selected piece can still advance right from this pocket/corridor toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 8: MOVE_RIGHT | Test whether moving into the adjacent wall causes any wrap/teleport or other nonstandard interaction that can reach the goal region.
history.reasoning: Test whether moving into the adjacent wall causes any wrap/teleport or other nonstandard interaction that can reach the goal region.

history.summary: Turn 9: MOVE_RIGHT | Even though the heuristic says a wall is adjacent, we need to probe for any non-standard wall behavior to escape this pocket toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: Even though the heuristic says a wall is adjacent, we need to probe for any non-standard wall behavior to escape this pocket toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 10: MOVE_RIGHT | Try to push the adjacent selectable to the right; if pushing is allowed it may slide into/through the wall gap toward the goal area.
history.reasoning: Try to push the adjacent selectable to the right; if pushing is allowed it may slide into/through the wall gap toward the goal area.

history.summary: Turn 11: CLICK | Switch to the other selectable on the right side to see if it can move around/push to access the goal region or open a path.
history.reasoning: Switch to the other selectable on the right side to see if it can move around/push to access the goal region or open a path.

history.summary: Turn 12: MOVE_UP | Try moving up into the goal area; goals are non-collidable so this may succeed even though it's flagged as blocked.
history.reasoning: Try moving up into the goal area; goals are non-collidable so this may succeed even though it's flagged as blocked.
